title: Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Rajasthan

overview.short: Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in Dholpur district, Rajasthan, India.
overview.description: Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in Dholpur district, Rajasthan, India. It was notified as a sanctuary in 1955 and covers an area of about 14.76 km².

topPlaces.name: Van Vihar Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Van Vihar Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in the Dholpur district of Rajasthan, India. Established in 1955, it spans approximately 26.6 square kilometers and is one of the oldest wildlife reserves in the region.

topPlaces.name: Ramsagar Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Ramsagar Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located in Dholpur district, in the Indian state of Rajasthan. It was notified as a sanctuary in 1955 under the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972 by the Rajasthan Government.

topPlaces.name: Lodi–Tomaras War
topPlaces.description: The Lodi–Tomaras War (1501–1507) was a series of military campaigns fought between Sultan Sikandar Lodi of the Delhi Sultanate and Man Singh Tomar of the Tomaras of Gwalior. Sikandar captured Dholpur from Vinayak Deva in 1501.

topPlaces.name: Dholpur state
topPlaces.description: Dholpur State or Dhaulpur State, ruled by Bamrolia Jat Dynasty was an independent kingdom from 1805 to 1818 and a princely state under British suzerainty from 1818 to 1949 in eastern Rajasthan. It was founded by Rana Kirat Singh, the ruler of Gohad, in 1805 AD. Dholpur State was a salute state entitled to a 15 gun-salute official and 17 gun-salute Personal.

topPlaces.name: Dholpur
topPlaces.description: Dholpur is a city in the Dholpur district in Rajasthan state of India. Dholpur is situated on the left bank of the Chambal river.

topPlaces.name: Tagawali
topPlaces.description: Tagawali is a village in Rajakhera Mandal (Panchayat Samiti), Dholpur district, Rajasthan state, India. Tagawali is 2 km far from its District Main City Dholpur and 300 km far from its State capital Jaipur.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Van Vihar Wildlife Sanctuary & around

items.note: Van Vihar Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in the Dholpur district of Rajasthan, India. Established in 1955, it spans approximately 26.6 square kilometers and is one of the oldest wildlife reserves in the region.

Ramsagar Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located in Dholpur district, in the Indian state of Rajasthan. It was notified as a sanctuary in 1955 under the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972 by the Rajasthan Government.

The Lodi–Tomaras War (1501–1507) was a series of military campaigns fought between Sultan Sikandar Lodi of the Delhi Sultanate and Man Singh Tomar of the Tomaras of Gwalior. Sikandar captured Dholpur from Vinayak Deva in 1501.

Dholpur State or Dhaulpur State, ruled by Bamrolia Jat Dynasty was an independent kingdom from 1805 to 1818 and a princely state under British suzerainty from 1818 to 1949 in eastern Rajasthan. It was founded by Rana Kirat Singh, the ruler of Gohad, in 1805 AD. Dholpur State was a salute state entitled to a 15 gun-salute official and 17 gun-salute Personal.
Dholpur is a city in the Dholpur district in Rajasthan state of India. Dholpur is situated on the left bank of the Chambal river.

Tagawali is a village in Rajakhera Mandal (Panchayat Samiti), Dholpur district, Rajasthan state, India. Tagawali is 2 km far from its District Main City Dholpur and 300 km far from its State capital Jaipur.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (57 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (58 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kesarbagh Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Van Vihar Wildlife Sanctuary, Ramsagar Wildlife Sanctuary, Lodi–Tomaras War, Dholpur state, Dholpur.
